name: BoxyHQ SAML Jackson Enterprise SSO API
what_it_does: Open-source SAML 2.0 and OIDC SSO service (SAML Jackson) that B2B SaaS companies self-host or use as a cloud service to add enterprise SSO to their products without building the SAML implementation from scratch.
use_cases: Adding SAML enterprise SSO to B2B SaaS without building SAML from scratch
Self-hosting SSO infrastructure for data residency or regulatory compliance
Normalizing SAML and OIDC identity providers to a single OAuth2 interface
Managing enterprise SSO connections per customer tenant via REST API
Directory sync (SCIM) for automated user provisioning from enterprise IdPs
not_for: Teams needing fully-managed cloud SSO without self-hosting infrastructure
best_when: A B2B SaaS team wants open-source SAML SSO they can self-host for full control, with an API that normalizes SAML/OIDC to standard OAuth2.
avoid_when: You need a fully-managed SSO solution without self-hosting — use WorkOS or Scalekit instead.

auth.notes: API key authentication for SSO connection management. The service itself acts as an OAuth2 authorization server for downstream apps. Tenant-scoped connection management via tenant/product identifiers.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Open source: completely free to self-host; cloud from $25/month
pricing.paid_tiers: BoxyHQ cloud from $25/month; Enterprise custom; self-hosted free forever
pricing.notes: Apache 2.0 open source — fully free to self-host. Cloud service for teams that don't want to manage infrastructure. Unique open-source positioning vs WorkOS/Scalekit which are cloud-only.

performance.rate_limits: Self-hosted: limited by your infrastructure; cloud: documented limits

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Good documentation for self-hosting and API usage. REST API documented for SSO connection management. Error responses are JSON. Cloud and self-hosted versions have slightly different configuration patterns.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: SSO connection upsert by tenant+product is idempotent. Directory sync operations depend on IdP SCIM implementation. Read operations are always idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: Self-hosted requires database (PostgreSQL, MySQL, or MongoDB) and operational knowledge
SAML metadata XML from customer IdP required before SSO can be configured
Tenant and product identifiers must be consistent — mismatches cause auth failures
Cloud vs self-hosted API behavior can differ — test against target deployment

security_score_components.security_notes: Apache 2.0 open source — full security audit possible. SAML signature verification and encryption. Self-hosted means complete data sovereignty. TLS required for production deployments. SOC2 for cloud service.
